evals.prompt: Create a new ProductCard.svelte component that displays a product thumbnail, title, price, and countdown timer. It should match the dark theme, have a hover effect with the GlowingEffect border, and be responsive — stack vertically on mobile, show as a grid card on desktop.
evals.expected_output: A Svelte 5 component using $props(), existing design tokens (--color-surface, --color-border, etc.), .card-bh class, GlowingEffect integration, responsive layout. Visual verification via Playwriter showing dark theme compliance at mobile and desktop viewports.
expectations: Component uses $props() with explicit TypeScript types
Uses existing CSS variables, not hardcoded colors
Uses .card-bh or equivalent dark-theme styling
Includes GlowingEffect component for hover border
Responsive: different layout at mobile vs desktop
Includes aria attributes on decorative elements
Visual loop was run with screenshots taken

evals.prompt: Add a subtle floating particle effect to the homepage background. Small white dots that drift slowly upward with slight horizontal oscillation. Keep the dark background. Make sure it respects prefers-reduced-motion.
evals.expected_output: A Three.js component or modification using RawShaderMaterial or PointsMaterial, proper cleanup and disposal, reduced motion check, WebGL fallback. Visual verification showing particles on dark background.
expectations: Uses dynamic import for Three.js
Checks prefers-reduced-motion before animating
Checks WebGL availability
Disposes all GPU resources on destroy
Canvas has aria-hidden=true and pointer-events: none
Pixel ratio limited to Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2)
Visual loop was run with screenshot verification

evals.prompt: The product listing page has too much spacing between cards on mobile and the filter bar wraps awkwardly. Fix the responsive layout and add smooth fade-in animations when products load using the existing animate-in pattern.
evals.expected_output: Responsive fixes to the product listing page with proper grid/flex adjustments for mobile, scroll-triggered animations using the animate-in/visible class pattern with IntersectionObserver. Visual verification at 375px viewport showing no awkward wrapping.
expectations: Uses existing .animate-in and .visible CSS classes
Uses IntersectionObserver for scroll-triggered reveals
Mobile layout fixed at 375px width
No backend files modified
Uses existing Tailwind breakpoint utilities
Visual loop was run at multiple viewports
